lesson.name: Academic and Technical Writing - Lesson 2 — Technical Composition

tasks.chapterTitle: Definition and Scope of Technical Composition

messages.content:
### Summary: Introduction to Technical Composition

Technical composition is a specialized field of communication focused on conveying complex information in professional and technical environments.

**Key Definitions**
The Society for Technical Communication defines technical communication as any content that fulfills at least one of the following criteria:
*   **Subject Matter:** Addresses specialized topics such as engineering, medicine, finance, or biotechnology [S.1].
*   **Medium:** Utilizes technology as a delivery platform, including web pages, social media, or digital help documentation [S.1].
*   **Instructional Purpose:** Provides guidance on how to complete a specific task, regardless of how technical that task may be [S.1].

**Scope and Application**
Technical writing is the primary subfield of technical communication. It is utilized across a vast array of industries—including computer hardware, aeronautics, robotics, and forestry—to create clear, structured documentation for professional use [S.1].

Lesson 2 — Technical Composition - Presentation5 slides
Lesson 2: Technical Composition
  • Defining the scope of professional documentation.
  • Understanding functional writing standards.

### Summary of Technical Composition

Technical composition represents the primary subfield of technical communication, encompassing professional writing across diverse sectors such as engineering, healthcare, finance, and technology.

**Key Definitions and Criteria**
According to the Society for Technical Communication, a document qualifies as technical communication if it satisfies at least one of the following conditions:

*   **Subject Matter:** The content addresses specialized or technical topics, including software, medical procedures, or regulatory guidelines.
*   **Medium:** The communication is delivered via technological platforms, such as online documentation, web pages, or social media.
*   **Instructional Purpose:** The content provides clear instructions for completing a specific task, regardless of how complex the subject might be.

This document provides a catalog of open-access educational resources for technical and professional writing. The resources are categorized by their respective source collections and are available via the LibreTexts Humanities library.

Key educational resources listed within the text include:

1. Foundational Guides and Compendiums
The texts include works such as Technical Communication by Milbourne et al., Effective Technical Writing in the Information Age by Schall, and Online Technical Writing by McMurrey [S.2]. Additionally, the collection features Open Technical Communication 3e by Reardon et al., and Open Technical Writing by Pope [S.3].

2. Specialized Instructional Materials
The list also highlights focused texts intended for professional development in the field, including Technical and Professional Writing by Jorgenson Borchert [S.3] and Technical Writing Essentials by Last [S.4].

3. Resource Access
All listed materials are curated under the LibreTexts Technical Composition bookshelf, which ensures that these texts remain accessible for instruction at no cost [S.2, S.3, S.4].
Here is a quiz for this lesson.

questions.question: Which of the following scenarios best satisfies the definition of technical communication based on the provided criteria?
options: A casual email written to a colleague about weekend plans.
A step-by-step guide explaining how to change a lightbulb in a lamp.
An opinion piece discussing the political implications of a new tax law.
A fictional short story set in a futuristic robotics workshop.
questions.explanation: According to the text, technical communication includes any document that provides instructions on how to complete a task, regardless of its technical complexity, such as changing a lightbulb.

questions.question: Why is a webpage considered a form of technical communication according to the Society for Technical Communication?
options: Because all webpages contain specialized medical or software information.
Because it is managed by the Society for Technical Communication.
Because it utilizes technology as a medium for communication.
Because webpages are exclusively designed for professional engineering fields.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that technical communication utilizes technology as a medium, including formats such as web pages, help documentation, or social media platforms.

questions.question: Based on the definition provided, which of these is the defining characteristic of technical writing?
options: It must be written exclusively by professionals in fields like chemistry or aeronautics.
It must meet at least one of the defined criteria involving subject matter, medium, or instructional utility.
It must use highly complex language that only experts in the specific industry can understand.
It is restricted solely to the fields of computer hardware and software.
questions.explanation: The text defines technical communication by whether it meets one or more of three specific criteria: subjects, medium, or the provision of instructions.

questions.question: If a writer creates a manual explaining the environmental regulations for a forestry project, which criterion for technical communication does this primarily fulfill?
options: It addresses a technical or specialized subject.
It is written exclusively for computer software engineers.
It is not considered technical communication because it is not a digital medium.
It provides instructions for a simple task rather than a specialized one.
questions.explanation: Environmental regulations are categorized as technical or specialized subjects, which is one of the three primary criteria for technical communication.

questions.question: How does the scope of 'Technical Composition' relate to the broader field of technical communication?
options: Technical composition is unrelated to technical communication.
Technical composition is the smallest subfield of technical communication.
Technical composition represents the largest subfield within technical communication.
Technical composition is only concerned with writing about robotics.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that technical writing (composition) represents the largest subfield within technical communication.

cards.front: What is the official definition of technical writing in a professional context?
cards.back: It is written content used in technical and professional fields, such as engineering, robotics, finance, and healthcare, representing the largest subfield of technical communication.

cards.front: List the three criteria established by the Society for Technical Communication for technical communication.
cards.back: 1) Addresses technical/specialized subjects. 2) Utilizes technology as a medium. 3) Provides instructions on how to complete a task.

cards.front: Provide three examples of 'technical or specialized subjects' according to the STC definition.
cards.back: Computer software, medical procedures, and environmental regulations.

cards.front: What are three examples of technology used as a medium for technical communication?
cards.back: Web pages, help documentation, and social media platforms.

cards.front: What is the primary factor regarding task completion that qualifies as technical communication?
cards.back: Providing instructions on how to complete a task, regardless of its level of technical complexity.

cards.front: Which group defines the criteria for what constitutes technical communication?
In which professional field would writing about 'forestry' or 'biotechnology' be categorized?
Is technical writing limited only to complex tasks? Why or why not?
cards.back: No, it is not limited to complexity; if the content provides instructions on how to complete a task, it meets the criteria for technical communication.

cards.front: What is the relationship between technical writing and technical communication?
cards.back: Technical writing is the largest subfield within the broader category of technical communication.

cards.front: Name two specific professional fields, other than IT or engineering, where technical composition is vital.

bullets: Defining the scope of professional documentation.
slides.notes: Welcome to our second lesson, where we explore the essential principles of technical composition and its application in professional fields.

slides.title: Defining Technical Composition
bullets: Covers engineering, chemistry, healthcare, and robotics.
Addresses specialized subjects and technical regulations.
Focuses on instructions and technology-driven mediums.
slides.notes: Technical composition spans diverse industries, creating content that simplifies complex information through clear, task-oriented writing.

slides.title: Key Communication Resources (1/3)
bullets: Technical and Report Writing (Kinonen et al.).
Effective Technical Writing in the Information Age (Schall).
slides.notes: These foundational texts provide established frameworks for mastering professional and technical style in the modern digital landscape.

slides.title: Key Communication Resources (2/3)
slides.notes: Open-access resources offer a wealth of instructional material tailored to the evolving needs of technical writers today.

slides.title: Key Communication Resources (3/3)
slides.notes: Rounding out our reading list, these essential texts finalize our overview of the core literature in technical composition.
